name: sidebar
title: Sidebar
description: App sidebar with icon-collapse + mobile off-canvas. Converted from shadcn ui/sidebar.tsx (SidebarProvider/useSidebar/useIsMobile context stack, ~720 lines) — open/collapsed state lives as a data-state attribute read via Tailwind group-data selectors (no context); mobile vs desktop is CSS display, not a JS media query (useIsMobile deleted): both the desktop aside and a mobile Sheet wrapping the same slotted content render in the DOM, exactly one is ever visible. variant prop (sidebar | floating | inset) matches shadcn's three looks; inset drives SidebarInset's own margin/rounding via a peer/peer-data-[variant=inset] relationship. Five files: Sidebar, SidebarGroup, SidebarMenuButton, SidebarTrigger, SidebarInset.

files.content:
--- /** * Sidebar.astro -- astro-shad app sidebar. Converted from shadcn * ui/sidebar.tsx (the SidebarProvider/Sidebar/useIsMobile/useSidebar * stack, ~720 lines). React's job there was almost entirely state * plumbing (open/collapsed/mobile) through context -- all three collapse * to DOM state here: * * - open/collapsed lives as `data-state` on this component's own root, * read by children via `group-data-[state=collapsed]/sidebar:*` * (Tailwind arbitrary group variant) -- no context, no prop drilling. * - mobile vs desktop is NOT a JS media-query (useIsMobile deleted * entirely): both the desktop `
` (`hidden md:flex`) and a mobile * `` wrapping the SAME slotted content (`md:hidden`) render in * the DOM; the breakpoint is CSS `display`, so exactly one is ever * visible. `header`/default/`footer` slot content is needed in BOTH * places -- literally repeating `` tags for this turned out to be * unreliable in practice (Astro's "render a slot again" escape hatch, * `data-astro-rerun`, plus multiple same-named-slot siblings on the * caller side, hit several compounding edge cases -- astro#17266 and * related). Sidestepped entirely: each slot is rendered to an HTML * string exactly ONCE via `Astro.slots.render()` in the frontmatter * below, then `set:html` puts that string DIRECTLY on the real wrapper * `
` in both the desktop and mobile markup -- no `` tag * appears more than once anywhere in this file, and no `` * indirection either (`set:html` on a `` proved unreliable * paired with `Astro.slots.render()` -- a real DOM node is what * `set:html` wants). Each wrapper only renders when its slot actually * has content (`{headerHtml &&
}`) -- replaces the * `empty:hidden` CSS trick, which doesn't reliably catch this case * (line-break whitespace between tags can keep a div out of `:empty`). * Trade-off, stated plainly: header/content/footer content still exists * TWICE in the HTML (once per branch) -- fine for nav markup (links, * buttons), a poor fit for anything with client state or a * crypto.randomUUID()-based id that must be unique per page (e.g. two * instances with the same name would each render twice). * - `SidebarTrigger` drives both branches from one click: it flips this * root's `data-state`, then click()s the mobile Sheet's own hidden * trigger span so Sheet's existing open logic runs unchanged. Harmless * on desktop -- the whole mobile branch is `md:hidden`, so the Sheet * opening there is invisible. * * `variant` ("sidebar" default | "floating" | "inset") matches shadcn's * three looks: "sidebar" is a flush edge-to-edge rail with a border; * "floating" adds padding around a rounded/ring/shadow inner card; * "inset" looks the same as "sidebar" on its own but tells `SidebarInset` * (via a `peer`/`peer-data-[variant=inset]` relationship -- see below) to * render the main content as its own inset rounded card instead of full * width. The draggable resize `SidebarRail` (shadcn's collapse-by-drag * handle) is still dropped -- decorative, not core, addable later if a * design calls for it. * * "inset"'s illusion (one continuous page, a white card floating inset * on all four sides) depends on a PAGE-LEVEL choice this component can't * make for you: give your own outer wrapper `bg-sidebar` (not the * default `bg-background`), so the color around `SidebarInset`'s margin * matches the `
` itself -- the aside then visually blends into * "the page" and only `SidebarInset`'s card (still `bg-background`) * reads as distinct. Collapsed, this is also why the aside appears to * "disappear": it's still there physically (an icon rail, or nothing at * all under `collapsible="offcanvas"`), just the same flat color as * everything around it. * *
* ... * ... *
* `collapsible="none"` renders an always-expanded, non-collapsing aside * (no mobile Sheet needed either, since there's nothing to toggle). * * The `peer`/`peer-data-*` link to `SidebarInset` needs a REAL DOM * sibling, not the inner `
` -- this component's own root * (`data-astro-shad-sidebar-root`) is `display:contents` precisely so it * doesn't add a layout box, but it's still the literal DOM sibling of * `` in consumer markup, so `peer`/`data-variant`/ * `data-state` live there. `SidebarTrigger` keeps that root's * `data-state` in sync with the `
`'s on every toggle (both are * updated together), so `SidebarInset`'s `peer-data-[state=collapsed]` * margin adjustment (inset variant only) never goes stale. * *
* *
...brand...
* * * Create * * *
...user menu...
* * * * ...page content... * *
* * `header`/`footer` are raw slots -- Sidebar.astro imposes no structure on * them (unlike `SidebarMenuButton`'s dedicated `icon` slot + auto-hiding * label). If your header/footer content is a brand row or a user row * (mark + text label), apply the SAME collapse convention * `SidebarMenuButton` uses internally, by hand: leave the mark (svg / img * / styled span badge -- whatever it is) bare so it keeps showing at any * width, and add `class="group-data-[state=collapsed]/sidebar:hidden"` * to the text label so it disappears instead of wrapping/smushing in the * 3rem icon rail. A one-class convention beats a dedicated slot API here * since it works identically regardless of what the mark element is. * *
* A * astro-shad *
* * Need more than one piece of content in `footer` (e.g. a quick-links * group ABOVE a user row)? Put both inside one `
` rather than giving each element its own * `slot="footer"` -- several separate same-named-slot siblings hits an * open Astro edge case (astro#17266) where only one ends up rendering. * A real DOM node (not ``) matters here too -- this repo's * `Astro.slots.render()` usage above only picked up a Fragment-wrapped * slot's content unreliably; `class="contents"` (`display: contents`) * keeps the wrapper out of the layout tree, so it costs nothing visually: * *
* ... *
...user row...
*
*/ import { cn } from "../../lib/utils"; import Sheet from "./Sheet.astro"; interface Props { side?: "left" | "right"; variant?: "sidebar" | "floating" | "inset"; collapsible?: "icon" | "offcanvas" | "none"; defaultOpen?: boolean; class?: string; [key: string]: unknown; } const { side = "left", variant = "sidebar", collapsible = "icon", defaultOpen = true, class: className, ...rest } = Astro.props; const state = defaultOpen ? "expanded" : "collapsed"; const padded = variant === "floating" || variant === "inset"; /* Every branch must appear as a complete literal string in this file for Tailwind's static scanner to pick it up -- a template-literal-built class name would never match. */ const COLLAPSE_CLASS = collapsible === "offcanvas" ? "data-[state=collapsed]:w-0 data-[state=collapsed]:overflow-hidden data-[state=collapsed]:border-transparent data-[state=collapsed]:p-0" : padded ? "data-[state=collapsed]:w-[calc(var(--sidebar-width-icon)+1rem)]" : "data-[state=collapsed]:w-(--sidebar-width-icon)"; const SIDE_BORDER_CLASS = padded ? "" : side === "left" ? "border-r border-sidebar-border" : "border-l border-sidebar-border"; /* Render each slot to an HTML string exactly once -- see the header comment. Reused via set:html in both the desktop and mobile markup below instead of ever writing more than once. */ const headerHtml = await Astro.slots.render("header"); const contentHtml = await Astro.slots.render("default"); const footerHtml = await Astro.slots.render("footer"); ---
{headerHtml &&
} { contentHtml && (
) } {footerHtml &&
}
{ collapsible !== "none" && (
{headerHtml &&
} {contentHtml && (
)} {footerHtml &&
}
) }

--- /** * SidebarInset.astro -- astro-shad main-content wrapper for a `` * layout. Converted from shadcn's SidebarInset. When the sibling * `` is used, this renders as its own inset * rounded/shadowed card (margin, `rounded-xl`, `shadow-sm`) instead of * full width -- driven by `peer-data-[variant=inset]` reading * `Sidebar.astro`'s root `data-variant` (a real DOM sibling, see that * file's header comment for why `peer` lives there and not on the * `
`). `peer-data-[state=collapsed]` narrows the left margin * further once the sidebar collapses, kept in sync by `SidebarTrigger`. * * No explicit `height` here (no `h-full`) -- this is a flex item of the * consumer's flex row, and `height:100%` sits OUTSIDE the margin box * (margin is added on top of 100%, not subtracted from it), so a `h-full` * element carrying the inset variant's `m-2` would overflow its * container by the margin amount, clipping the bottom rounded corner * against any `overflow-hidden` ancestor. Flexbox's default * `align-items:stretch` sizes an unset-height item to the container's * cross-axis size MINUS its own margin automatically -- the fix is * simply not fighting that with an explicit percentage height. * *
* ... * * * ...page content... * *
*/ import { cn } from '../../lib/utils'; interface Props { class?: string; [key: string]: unknown; } const { class: className, ...rest } = Astro.props; ---
